intermediate_mechanisms: Reduced H6PDH activity lowers endoplasmic-reticulum NADPH regeneration required for 11beta-HSD1 oxoreductase activity.
Reduced cortisol regeneration increases cortisol clearance and hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal-axis drive.
ACTH-mediated adrenal stimulation can be accompanied by adrenal hyperplasia on imaging.
Excess adrenal androgen exposure accelerates adrenarche.
Excess adrenal androgen exposure causes precocious pseudopuberty.
Adrenal androgen excess disrupts menstrual cycling.
Adrenal androgen excess disrupts menstrual cycling.
Hyperandrogenic reproductive dysfunction can impair fertility.
Premature androgen exposure accelerates growth and bone maturation.
Premature androgen exposure accelerates growth and bone maturation.
ACTH-driven adrenal steroidogenesis alters steroid precursor concentrations.
ACTH-driven adrenal steroidogenesis alters mineralocorticoid precursor concentrations.
Abnormal deoxycorticosterone and mineralocorticoid precursor activity promote sodium retention and volume expansion.
Mineralocorticoid receptor activation promotes renal potassium wasting.
Mineralocorticoid-mediated volume expansion suppresses circulating renin.

edges.description: Decreased cortisone-to-cortisol regeneration reports the defining enzymatic defect in CRD/ACRD.
Abnormal cortisone handling and urinary steroid ratios report impaired conversion of cortisone to cortisol.
Increased circulating androgens report the ACTH-mediated adrenal hyperandrogenism branch of CRD.
Elevated 11-deoxycortisol reports the adrenal steroid-profile disturbance accompanying ACTH-mediated steroidogenesis.

nodes.description: Inadequate cortisol regeneration stimulates hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal activation and adrenal cortical steroidogenesis, producing adrenal androgen excess and related reproductive and developmental phenotypes.

evidence.reference_title: Hyperandrogenism due to cortisone reductase deficiency
evidence.snippet: A rare, genetic, endocrine disease characterized by defect in conversion of cortisone to active cortisol, resulting in ACTH-mediated excessive androgen release from adrenal glands.

evidence.reference_title: Alterations of Cortisol Metabolism in Human Disorders.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in the corresponding HSD11B1 gene, or in the H6PD gene encoding hexose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase (which supplies the NADPH required for the oxoreductase activity of 11-HSD1), cause apparent cortisone reductase deficiency, a rare syndrome of adrenocortical hyperactivity and hyperandrogenism.

nodes.description: Altered deoxycorticosterone is listed as a frequent steroid-profile phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Hyperandrogenism due to cortisone reductase deficiency
evidence.snippet: HP:0031186 | Abnormal circulating deoxycorticosterone level | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Accelerated skeletal maturation is part of the male premature-androgen-exposure phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Hyperandrogenism due to cortisone reductase deficiency
evidence.snippet: proportionate tall stature and accelerated bone maturation in males

nodes.description: Central obesity is reported among female manifestations in the Orphanet definition.

evidence.snippet: hirsutism, oligoamenorrhea, central obesity and infertility in females.

evidence.reference_title: Alterations of Cortisol Metabolism in Human Disorders.
evidence.snippet: a rare syndrome of adrenocortical hyperactivity and hyperandrogenism.

evidence.snippet: In cortisone reductase deficiency (CRD), activation of cortisone to cortisol does not occur

evidence.snippet: HP:0003351 | Decreased circulating renin concentration | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Low-dose dexamethasone can suppress ACTH-driven adrenal androgen excess in apparent cortisone reductase deficiency. Hydrocortisone may be ineffective in ACRD because it is rapidly converted to cortisone.

evidence.reference_title: A follow-up history of young man with apparent cortisone reductase deficiency (ACRD) - several years after diagnosis.
evidence.snippet: Control laboratory studies indicated suppression of excess adrenal androgen synthesis, but we never got the THF + allo-THF/THE ratio in normal values.

evidence.reference_title: A follow-up history of young man with apparent cortisone reductase deficiency (ACRD) - several years after diagnosis.
evidence.snippet: Hydrocortisone treatment is ineffective in ACRD patients because it was rapidly metabolized to cortisone.

evidence.reference_title: 11beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 1 deficiency ('apparent cortisone reductase deficiency') in a 6-year-old boy.
evidence.snippet: Treatment with dexamethasone (0.375 mg/daily) resulted in androgen suppression.

evidence.reference_title: Novel H6PDH mutations in two girls with premature adrenarche: 'apparent' and 'true' CRD can be differentiated by urinary steroid profiling.
evidence.snippet: Case 1 was compound heterozygous for R109AfsX3 and a novel P146L missense mutation in H6PD. Case 2 was compound heterozygous for novel nonsense mutations Q325X and Y446X in H6PD.

evidence.reference_title: Steroid biomarkers and genetic studies reveal inactivating mutations in hexose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase in patients with cortisone reductase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Three cases were identified as homozygous (R109AfsX3, Y316X, and G359D) and one case identified as compound heterozygous (c.960G-->A and D620fsX3) for mutations in H6PD.

nodes.description: H6PD loss of function reduces NADPH generation in the endoplasmic reticulum, which is required to drive 11beta-HSD1 in the cortisone-to-cortisol oxoreductase direction.

evidence.reference_title: Novel H6PDH mutations in two girls with premature adrenarche: 'apparent' and 'true' CRD can be differentiated by urinary steroid profiling.
evidence.snippet: H6PDH generates cofactor NADPH for 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 1 (11β-HSD1, encoded by HSD11B1) oxo-reductase activity, converting cortisone to cortisol.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the genes encoding 11beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 1 and hexose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase interact to cause cortisone reductase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in exon 5 of H6PD in individuals with CRD attenuated or abolished H6PDH activity.

evidence.reference_title: Steroid biomarkers and genetic studies reveal inactivating mutations in hexose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase in patients with cortisone reductase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Expression and activity assays demonstrate loss of function for all reported H6PDH mutations.

evidence.reference_title: Cortisone-reductase deficiency associated with heterozygous mutations in 11beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 1.
evidence.snippet: Novel heterozygous mutations (R137C or K187N) were found in the coding sequence of HSD11B1.

nodes.description: HSD11B1 variants reduce 11beta-HSD1 abundance, dimer assembly, or catalytic activity, directly impairing cortisone-to-cortisol reduction.

evidence.reference_title: Cortisone-reductase deficiency associated with heterozygous mutations in 11beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 1.
evidence.snippet: Novel heterozygous mutations (R137C or K187N) were found in the coding sequence of HSD11B1.

evidence.reference_title: Cortisone-reductase deficiency associated with heterozygous mutations in 11beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 1.
evidence.snippet: Expression of either mutant in a bacterial system greatly reduced the yield of soluble protein, suggesting that both mutations interfere with subunit folding or dimer assembly.

nodes.description: The common biochemical lesion is reduced reduction of inactive cortisone to active cortisol in peripheral glucocorticoid target tissues, increasing cortisol clearance and weakening tissue glucocorticoid regeneration.

evidence.reference_title: Cortisone-reductase deficiency associated with heterozygous mutations in 11beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 1.
evidence.snippet: In peripheral target tissues, levels of active glucocorticoid hormones are controlled by 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 1 (11β-HSD1), a dimeric enzyme that catalyzes the reduction of cortisone to cortisol within the endoplasmic reticulum.

evidence.reference_title: Alterations of Cortisol Metabolism in Human Disorders.
evidence.snippet: The 11-HSD1 isozyme functions mainly as an oxoreductase (cortisone to cortisol) and is expressed at high levels in the liver and other glucocorticoid target tissues.
